name: rad-planner
description:
**Plan a project before you write any code — or replan after a pivot, deepen an existing plan, or check for drift.** rad-planner walks you through a five-phase plan-first conversation with project-specific doc recommendations, then writes the approved doc set as the plan's first executed milestone. The plan is the deliverable; the docs follow the plan, not the other way around.

`/plan` runs four entry points (full / improvement / drift / pivot) detected from utterance + project state, with a universal four-direction menu as the ambiguity fallback. Each entry routes through the five-phase conversation (or a subset):

- M1 Constitution & Frame — operating-manual content (Project, Read order, Hard boundaries, Engineering rules, Definition of done, Escalate triggers)
- M2 Goal-Backward Scope — must-be-TRUE / must-EXIST / CRITICAL vs nice-to-have, hardest unknown, derailment risks, non-goals (GSD-flavored scaffold)
- M3 Sequence with Size Discipline — milestones with 2-3 tasks each, ~50% context bar, dependencies mapped, risk-first ordering
- M4 Quality Gates — per-milestone acceptance criteria, validation commands, stop conditions, notes-for-next-session, project-wide Definition of Done
- M5 Doc-Set Recommendation — complexity routing (lite / standard / full), project-specific rationale per doc, user_approval hard gate before any writes

M6 executes the plan's first milestone: writes the approved doc set per the canonical structure — operating manual (CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/vision.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/planning/current.md`, `docs/status.md` scaffold (rad-session populates from evidence), `docs/decisions/`, optional `docs/roadmap.md`, `.rad/profile`. Guard rails protect existing user content — overwrite/append/skip prompts for pre-existing strategic docs, `/init` residue enriched not overwritten, code never touched.

Mode flags: `--full` (greenfield), `--improve` (deepen or continue existing plan), `--drift` (assessment report, not new plan), `--pivot` (substantial re-plan with disposition manifest), `--validate` (utility — run validators only). Plus `--agents claude_only|codex_only|claude_and_codex`, `--non-interactive`, `--resume <run-id>`, `--output-dir <path>`.

Four mechanical validators back the conversational workflow:
- `plan-lint.py` — required-section presence, AC checkbox format, vague-language detection on `planning/current.md`
- `status-validator.py` — freshness vs git mtime, 8-section presence, evidence-based validation results on `status.md`
- `doc-redundancy.py` — cross-doc bullet duplicate detection via Jaccard similarity
- `doc-contradiction.py` — vision.md non-goals vs current.md acceptance criteria via stemmed token overlap

Plus `validate-json.py` for subagent JSON contracts (unchanged from 3.0). Two test fixtures under `fixtures/` exercise the validators end-to-end.

Always-teaches in `/plan` — every phase explains why it asks what it asks; every doc recommendation includes project-specific rationale. Mentor vs dev mode (project-scoped in `.rad/profile`) gates teaching depth at `/wrapup` (via rad-session) but not at `/plan`.

Aligned with the published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research: three-band project memory model (operating manual / project memory / execution state), intent-vs-reality split between `planning/current.md` (plan) and `status.md` (evidence), AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md as conditional operating manuals based on agent scope. Canonical spec at [`docs/doc-conventions.md`](https://github.com/radesjardins/RAD-Claude-Skills/blob/main/docs/doc-conventions.md).

Pairs naturally with rad-session for the full project lifecycle, but works standalone. Migration tooling from 3.0 not included — neither prior version had public traffic.
